pattern: Treating the string like nested insertions, a stack that collapses every completed abc block validates the word exactly when the stack empties.
hints: The only thing that can ever close is the most recently opened abc, which is just like matching brackets.
Push characters and, whenever the top of your buffer becomes the sequence a then b then c, remove all three.
Scan left to right pushing letters onto a stack; on seeing c, pop only if the two items below are b then a, otherwise it is invalid, and the word is valid exactly when the stack ends empty.

edge_inputs.description: leftover letters keep the stack non-empty

stress.description: build a valid string by random nested insertions of abc, sometimes corrupt it
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(1, min(size // 3, 200))
    s = ''
    for _ in range(m):
        pos = rng.randint(0, len(s))
        s = s[:pos] + 'abc' + s[pos:]
    if rng.random() < 0.5 and len(s) >= 2:
        i = rng.randint(0, len(s) - 1)
        j = rng.randint(0, len(s) - 1)
        lst = list(s)
        lst[i], lst[j] = lst[j], lst[i]
        s = ''.join(lst)
    if not s:
        s = 'abc'
    return [s]
stress.note: mix of provably valid and randomly corrupted strings
